steps.title: Trace the three parts
steps.content: The passage identifies community science as a research approach, lists benefits of that approach, and then mentions Bennett's butterfly study as a case in which the approach was used.

steps.title: Reject mismatched sequences
steps.content: Choice A wrongly makes study results central. Choice B invents public opinion about the approach. Choice C invents a history of development and future collaborative projects.

steps.title: Choose the research sequence
steps.content: The remaining choice matches the passage's movement from defining an approach, to naming its benefits, to giving a study that used it. The answer is D.
